voice_lines.text: Listen. The valley goes quiet before a predator moves. The insects stop. The birds lift. When you hear nothing — run.
voice_lines.context: NPC warns player when large predator is within 300m

voice_lines.text: Water. Always find water first. A river means fish, means animals coming to drink, means you are not alone — but it also means predators know where you are.
voice_lines.context: First time player approaches river zone — survival tip

sound_references.name: Heavy Footsteps
sound_references.use: T-Rex and large dinosaur footstep loop — low frequency ground impact

sound_references.name: R15-77-Jungle Night
sound_references.use: Forest biome night ambient loop — insects, apes, frogs

sound_references.name: Tropical Ambience Night Ecuador
sound_references.use: River zone night ambient — crickets, frogs, water nearby

audio_zones.label: AudioZone_River_001
audio_zones.description: River ambient — water flow + frogs + crickets

audio_zones.label: AudioZone_Forest_001
audio_zones.description: Dense forest — insects + wind through canopy

audio_zones.label: AudioZone_Savanna_001
audio_zones.description: Open savanna — wind + distant animal calls

adaptive_music_states: CALM — player alone, no threats detected — sparse percussion, wind, nature
ALERT — predator within 300m — tension build, low drone, faster heartbeat
COMBAT — active predator engagement — full percussion, urgent rhythm
DANGER — health critical — distorted heartbeat, minimal music, raw survival
NIGHT — darkness, reduced visibility — dense insect chorus, distant roars
DISCOVERY — new area or resource found — brief melodic resolution
audio_design_notes.silence_as_design: Silence is the most powerful sound cue. When ambient insects stop, player knows danger is near. This is the Walter Murch principle — what is NOT there tells the story.
audio_design_notes.footstep_system: Footstep sounds vary by surface (dirt, rock, water, grass) and creature weight. T-Rex footsteps cause low-frequency rumble felt before heard.
audio_design_notes.voice_line_philosophy: NPCs speak in short, practical sentences. No exposition dumps. Every line contains actionable survival information or environmental context.
